What is Trademark Registration?

The purpose of registering a trademark is to protect the distinct identity of your logo, slogan and brand. With BookMyTM you can find a most convenient, hassle free, cost effective way to register a Trademark from anywhere in India.

Developing and establishing a business/brand undergoes lot of stress, pain and after all these you need to protect your brand name, logo, tagline, writing style, combination of colors, shape (3 dimensional shape) even sound or anything else that provides your brand a distinctive identity. Trademark registrations in India are based on Trademark Act 1999, which makes the provision for exclusive ownership rights and restrict others from usage of your brand name/business mark.

Application for logo registration can be done quickly and online. One can start using a “TM” symbol as soon as the application is made. However, TM registration process takes up to 6-18 months time. It includes various processes for validating original ownership of the mark. Trademark registration in India is going through many innovative changes. Consider this – one can now avail trademark for Unconventional Marks through which, even a distinct sound can now be registered as a trademark in India.

Why Register Your Trademark?
Safeguarded Commercial Goodwill

Registered owner of a Trademark has the right to create, establish and protect the goodwill of their products/services. The registered owner of a trademark can stop anyone else from unlawful usage of his Trademark. With Registered Trademark, one can sue a petition for stoppage or dilution of infringed use or to claim for the damages caused by the infringement legally.
Enhanced Reach of Goods and Services
A product or a company with registered Trademark will get a distinguished value in the market. Most of the companies reserve their unique identity by registering a trademark.
Legal Protection

By registering a Trademark, the owner reserves the exclusive right to use the same and he/she can sue a legal petition incase finds an infringement from anybody. Without registration of the trademark in case of infringement you may end up losing your original mark.
An intangible Asset
A registered trademark is an intangible asset to its owner. Registered Trademark can be sold, assigned, franchised or commercially contracted to bring benefits.

Frequently Asked Questions

items.q: Is there a Trademark office in Kochi or Trivandrum?
items.a: No. The appropriate Intellectual Property office for Kerala is located in Chennai (Guindy). However, all filings are done online, so your physical location in Kerala does not matter.

items.q: Can I use my Kerala Shop & Establishment license for Trademark?
items.a: Yes. Your Panchayat, Municipality, or Corporation license (Shop & Establishment) is a valid document to prove your business address and 'Date of User' (the date you started business).

items.q: I am a freelancer in Kerala. Can I register a trademark?
items.a: Yes. Individuals can register a trademark in their own name. You do not need a company registration (Pvt Ltd or LLP) to own a brand.

items.q: Does a Kerala trademark protect my brand in Dubai (GCC)?
items.a: No. An Indian trademark is valid only within India. If you export to the GCC, you must file a separate application under the Madrid Protocol or directly in the UAE.

blocks.text: Should the trademark be filed in the individual’s name or company’s name?

Many start-ups register a Trademark on the founder’s name because the future of a start-up is always in doubt, whereas large businesses prefer to do so in the name of the company. Start up prefers to register the mark in the founder’s name because the trademark would be valid regardless of the state of the entity.
What cannot be registered as trademark?

Any mark that is identical or deceptively similar to an existing registered trademark or a trademark for which the application for registration is already in process, such trademarks cannot be registered. Also, a mark that would likely cause a deception or confusion, or that appears offensive may not be registered.
Period of validity of Trademark Registration? Can a trademark registration be renewed?

The registered trademark is valid for a period of 10 years from the date of filing of application for trademark. It can be renewed every 10 years, perpetually. In India, renewal request is to be filed within Six months before the expiry of the last registration of trademark.
What is class of trademark? How to find appropriate class?

Controller General of Patents, Designs, and Trademarks has classified goods and services under 45 classes. Your application must mention the class/classes that the goods/services represent. The trademarks are registered only under those classes.
Will the trademark be valid outside India?
The registration application filed under the Trademark Act, 1999 is verified and registered in India and it remains valid only in India.
Brand Name & Logo: 1 or 2 applications?
The ministry accepts application containing the Logo & Brand name as 1 application if both are submitted together in one image. Hence, the charge for a single application would apply.
How does a start-up or MSME qualify for a 50% rebate on Government fee?

If you have a valid certificate issued by Start-up India (an initiative by Government of India), or an MSME registration your start-up gets qualified to avail 50% rebate on Government fees for brand-name registration.
Can a multi-class application be filed in India?
Yes, one can apply for registration under multiple classes in one single application.
Can a registered trademark be amended later?

Yes, the owner can amend the registered mark as per the provision of Section 22 of the Trademarks Act. This allows the amendment of the mark if it does not amount to a substantial change in the character of the mark.
Is the trademark liable for removal on the ground of non-use?

Yes, a registered trademark can be removed because of non-use. If it is not used for a continuous period of five years from the date of registration of the trademark and that the application for removal is made after three months from the expiry of these five years, then it can be removed.
Jurisdiction for Trademark Registration in Kerala
A common misconception is that Kerala has its own Registry. In reality, all applications for trademark registration in Kerala fall under the Chennai Jurisdiction.

items: Chennai Office: Covers Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, and Lakshadweep.
Digital Filing: BookMyTM handles the entire process online. You do not need to visit Chennai.

blocks.text: Govt Support for Trademark Registration in Kerala
Registered startups in Kerala (KSUM) and MSMEs are eligible for significant subsidies on government fees.
50% Fee Rebate
Pay ₹4,500 instead of ₹9,000 with a valid Udyam or Startup India certificate.
Reimbursement Schemes
KSUM offers schemes to reimburse IP protection costs for eligible tech startups.
Why Kerala Businesses Need Protection?
Kerala’s unique industries face specific risks that only a registered trademark can solve.
Ayurveda & Wellness
Vital for "Ayurvedic Resorts" to stop competitors from using confusingly similar names that mislead tourists.
Spices & Exports
Mandatory for listing on global platforms like Amazon Global and for customs clearance in foreign ports.
Tourism Brands
Prevents aggregators or rivals from bidding on your resort or houseboat brand name in Google Ads.
